report_name: Artificial Intelligence Exacerbates Cybersecurity Risks in Global Supply Chains

threat_actors.name: AI-Leveraging Adversaries
threat_actors.description: A general class of threat actors, from ransomware groups to nation-states, who leverage Artificial Intelligence to enhance their attacks and target AI systems within global supply chains.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries use Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate highly convincing, personalized phishing emails at scale to make their attacks more effective.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries compromise AI models and libraries from public repositories like Hugging Face or PyTorch Hub to attack the supply chain.

attack_patterns.name: System Checks
attack_patterns.description: AI-created polymorphic malware constantly changes its signature to evade detection by static analysis and sandboxing, which involves performing system checks to detect analysis environments.

attack_patterns.name: Poison AI Model
attack_patterns.description: Attackers inject malicious data into the training set of an AI model to create a hidden backdoor or bias its outputs, targeting the AI systems themselves.

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries trick an LLM-based application into ignoring its original instructions and executing the attacker's commands.

attack_patterns.name: Craft Adversarial Data
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries craft specific inputs, known as adversarial inputs, that cause an AI model to misclassify data, such as tricking a visual inspection system on a manufacturing line.

malware.name: AI-Generated Polymorphic Malware
malware.description: Polymorphic malware created by AI that can constantly change its signature to evade detection by static analysis and sandboxing.

indicators.name: AI Model Download from Public Repository
indicators.pattern:
title: AI Model Download from Public Repository
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b6c-9d1a-2b3c4d5e6f7a
status: experimental
description: Detects the cloning of AI models from the public Hugging Face repository. While often legitimate, this activity should be monitored as these models can be considered untrusted code and a vector for supply chain attacks.
references:
  - https://www.forbes.com/sites/chuckbrooks/2026/05/22/the-growing-cybersecurity-risks-to-the-supply-chain-in-the-ai-era/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/git'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'clone'
      - 'https://huggingface.co'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the downloading of new AI models from public repositories like Hugging Face, which should be treated as untrusted code.

indicators.name: API Calls to Public AI Services
indicators.pattern:
title: API Calls to Public AI Services
id: 1f2e3d4c-5b6a-7890-1234-567890abcdef
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for common public AI service API endpoints. This can be used to baseline normal usage and alert on unusual call volumes, strange prompts, or calls originating from unexpected servers, which could indicate prompt injection or data exfiltration attempts.
references:
  - https://www.forbes.com/sites/chuckbrooks/2026/05/22/the-growing-cybersecurity-risks-to-the-supply-chain-in-the-ai-era/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.aml.t0051
level: informational
logsource:
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - 'api.openai.com'
      - 'api.anthropic.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous API calls to AI services like OpenAI or Anthropic by monitoring DNS queries for their API endpoints.

relationships.source_name: AI Model Download from Public Repository
API Calls to Public AI Services
